meaning: Al-Khwārizmī is central to algorithmic naming and transmission, not the sole origin of algorithms.
Algorism is historically tied to written arithmetic with Hindu-Arabic numerals; algorithm later generalizes.
Verbal and tabular procedures are algorithmic without being computer programs.
Numerals, tables, prose, and classifications shape what procedures can do.
Translation, teaching, and reception are part of procedural history.
The word history matters, but the procedural content matters too.
